query: Several shells from a dry storage lot show fine uneven splits, but there was no recent machine contact change.
note: Drying or storage stress is the intended cause; avoid jumping to compression just because the symptom is a crack.

query: Cracks appear after a low humidity hold, following natural shell lines rather than one fixed equipment direction.
note: Targets KI-001 because the evidence points to material stress from drying rather than a single station.

query: The last failed units have crushed-looking splits and the sizing gap log dropped below its normal window.
note: Sizing compression and guide spacing evidence should retrieve KI-002.

query: A feed-path jam was cleared, then the next sample showed shell splits near the constrained side.
note: Hard negative: mentions a crack, but the key clue is constrained feed compression rather than drying.

query: A shallow narrow scrape started after product moved across a rough transfer chute corner.
note: Transfer contact is the intended cause; this should not be labeled as a natural crack.

query: Several shells have a long line after moving through the handoff bridge; operators first blamed the knife station.
note: Hard negative: the words knife and trimming are distracting; the real cause is transfer hardware contact, so KI-003 is correct.

query: Many pieces from one sorter lane have a similar curved mark in almost the same orientation.
note: Hard negative: the mark may look like a crack, but repeated lane geometry points to sorter guide contact.

query: Right after a lane guide insert was replaced, units from that lane show matching straight surface cuts.
note: Targets KI-004 because consistency across a sorter lane is the main evidence.

query: Round dark openings are spread across one incoming supplier lot, and handling impact logs look normal.
note: Hard negative: hole-like damage could be impact, but the lot-level incoming pattern points to KI-005.

query: Inspection sees recessed voids on shells before the product reaches the current line section.
note: Targets KI-005 because the issue appears pre-existing and material or biological in origin.

query: A dark opening with broken shell edges appeared after a high drop at the hopper exit.
note: Hard negative: it may be described as a hole, but the drop impact evidence should retrieve KI-006.

query: After bulk agitation was increased, some shells have chipped missing areas that look like holes.
note: Targets KI-006 because collision or agitation is the causal clue.

query: The defect is a colored surface mark with no shell opening, and a belt section has visible residue.
note: Hard negative: visual station may flag an anomaly, but the evidence is transfer residue, not physical damage.

query: QA first suspected packaging label ink, but the marked shells were found before packing after operators used colored inspection gloves.
note: Hard negative: packaging words point toward KI-012, but the evidence is pre-pack contact with glove or tray residue, so KI-007 is correct.

query: Every few units show nearly the same print-shaped stain at the same position after passing one wheel.
note: Hard negative: similar wording to ink transfer, but repetition from one contact surface points to KI-008.

query: The same residue pattern repeats across a short run, suggesting one dirty fixture is stamping shells.
note: Targets KI-008 because repeated shape and position are stronger than random contamination.

query: A faint hairline crack appeared after roller pressure and belt tension were increased for a brittle lot.
note: Hard negative: both KI-002 and KI-009 involve pressure, but roller or belt setting evidence is KI-009.

query: The report mentions drying stress, but the fine split appears exactly after a roller pressure change and has a rubbed edge.
note: Hard negative: drying stress wording should not beat the roller pressure and rubbed-edge evidence for KI-009.

query: Short straight nicks appeared near one side immediately after the trimming blade was replaced.
note: Targets KI-010 because the blade-change timing and nick geometry match trimming contact.

query: After clearing a jam at the trimming station, nearby shells show shallow slices with clean edges.
note: Hard negative: it is a cut-like mark, but trimming station timing is more specific than generic transfer contact.

query: The operator called it an impact chip, but the tiny pinholes cluster by supplier date code and there are no scrape or drop clues.
note: Hard negative: impact-chip wording points at KI-006, but tiny supplier-linked pinholes target KI-011.

query: Small pin dots appear in one harvest lot; logs show no drop, scrape, guide, roller, blade, belt, or packaging event.
note: Targets KI-011 because the cause is shell weakness rather than handling impact.

query: It was first called belt residue, but the stain began only after open trays sat beside fresh carton labels.
note: Hard negative: it is print-like, but packaging or label contact should retrieve KI-012 instead of belt transfer.

query: A cosmetic dark smudge appears after cartons with wet labels touched the open tray area.
note: Targets KI-012 because the source is packaging material rather than line hardware.
